paper: Amodal Intra-class Instance Segmentation: Synthetic Datasets and Benchmark
claim: The paper introduces two new amodal datasets
evidence: which contain a total of over 267K images of intra-class occlusion scenarios

paper: Amodal Intra-class Instance Segmentation: Synthetic Datasets and Benchmark
claim: The approach outperforms fully supervised methods
evidence: experiments show that our weakly supervised approach outperforms the SOTA fully supervised methods

paper: Amodal Intra-class Instance Segmentation: Synthetic Datasets and Benchmark
claim: Layer priors design exhibits remarkable performance improvements
evidence: our layer priors design exhibits remarkable performance improvements in the case of intra-class occlusion in both synthetic and real images

paper: Image Amodal Completion: A Survey
claim: Image amodal completion aims to equip computers with human-like amodal completion functions
evidence: The main purpose of this survey is to provide an intuitive understanding of the research hotspots, key technologies and future trends in the field of image amodal completion.

paper: Amodal Ground Truth and Completion in the Wild
claim: Previous work relied on manual annotation for amodal segmentation ground truth
evidence: In previous work, the amodal segmentation ground truth on real images is usually predicted by manual annotaton and thus is subjective.

paper: Amodal Ground Truth and Completion in the Wild
claim: A new evaluation benchmark is constructed
evidence: This pipeline is used to construct an amodal completion evaluation benchmark, MP3D-Amodal, consisting of a variety of object categories and labels.

paper: Open-World Amodal Appearance Completion
claim: reasoning amodal completion
evidence: We term this capability reasoning amodal completion, where the system reconstructs the full appearance of the queried object based on the provided image and language query.

paper: Hyper-Transformer for Amodal Completion
claim: Traditional methods often rely on two-stage processes or additional information, leading to inefficiencies and potential error accumulation.
evidence: Learning shape priors is crucial for effective amodal completion, but traditional methods often rely on two-stage processes or additional information, leading to inefficiencies and potential error accumulation.

paper: Hyper-Transformer for Amodal Completion
claim: The framework uses a hyper transformer equipped with a dynamic convolution head to directly learn shape priors and accurately predict amodal masks.
evidence: This framework utilizes a hyper transformer equipped with a dynamic convolution head to directly learn shape priors and accurately predict amodal masks.

paper: Amodal Completion via Progressive Mixed Context Diffusion
claim: We overcome two technical challenges
evidence: how to be free of unwanted co-occurrence bias, which tends to regenerate similar occluders, and how to judge if an amodal completion has succeeded

paper: Amodal Completion via Progressive Mixed Context Diffusion
claim: Our method exhibits improved photorealistic completion results
evidence: compared to existing approaches in numerous successful completion cases

paper: PHAC: Promptable Human Amodal Completion
claim: Existing HAC models offer users limited control over the completed content
evidence: Conditional image generation methods are increasingly used in human-centric applications, yet existing human amodal completion (HAC) models offer users limited control over the completed content.

paper: PHAC: Promptable Human Amodal Completion
claim: PHAC introduces a new task that completes occluded human images while satisfying both visible appearance constraints and multiple user prompts
evidence: To address these limitations, we introduce promptable human amodal completion (PHAC), a new task that completes occluded human images while satisfying both visible appearance constraints and multiple user prompts.

paper: Integrating Multimodal Large Language Model Knowledge into Amodal Completion
claim: Existing approaches either depend solely on the image generation ability of visual generative models, which lack such knowledge, or leverage it only during the segmentation stage
evidence: Just as humans infer hidden regions based on prior experience and common sense, this task inherently requires physical knowledge about real-world entities.

paper: Integrating Multimodal Large Language Model Knowledge into Amodal Completion
claim: The proposed framework harnesses the real-world knowledge of Multimodal Large Language Models to guide amodal completion
evidence: To address this, we propose AmodalCG, a novel framework that harnesses the real-world knowledge of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to guide amodal completion.

paper: Amodal Completion and Size Constancy in Natural Scenes
claim: The authors propose to tackle these issues by building upon advances in object recognition and using recently created large-scale datasets.
evidence: We consider the problem of enriching current object detection systems with veridical object sizes and relative depth estimates from a single image.

paper: Amodal Completion and Size Constancy in Natural Scenes
claim: Amodal bounding box completion is introduced to infer the full extent of the object instances in the image.
evidence: We first introduce the task of amodal bounding box completion, which aims to infer the the full extent of the object instances in the image.

paper: Amodal Completion and Size Constancy in Natural Scenes
claim: Category-specific object size distributions can be learned from available annotations and used to infer veridical sizes in novel images.
evidence: We then propose a probabilistic framework for learning category-specific object size distributions from available annotations and leverage these in conjunction with amodal completion to infer veridical sizes in novel images.

paper: Amodal Completion and Size Constancy in Natural Scenes
claim: Focal length prediction can be used to overcome inherent scaling ambiguities by exploiting scene recognition.
evidence: Finally, we introduce a focal length prediction approach that exploits scene recognition to overcome inherent scaling ambiguities and we demonstrate qualitative results on challenging real-world scenes.

paper: Training for X-Ray Vision: Amodal Segmentation, Amodal Content Completion, and View-Invariant Object Representation from Multi-Camera Video
claim: MOVi-MC-AC is the largest amodal segmentation and first amodal content dataset to date
evidence: until now, no data has provided an additional dimension for object context: the possibility of multiple cameras sharing a view of a scene.

paper: Training for X-Ray Vision: Amodal Segmentation, Amodal Content Completion, and View-Invariant Object Representation from Multi-Camera Video
claim: The dataset includes multiple camera settings for object tracking between unique perspectives
evidence: MOVi-MC-AC provides consistent object ids for detections and segmentations between both frames and multiple cameras each with unique features and motion patterns on a single scene.
